new_classes.definition_en: A blended whisky produced in Scotland by blending one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, classified under CN 2208 30 71 (containers ≤ 2 L) or 2208 30 79 (containers > 2 L).

new_classes.label_en: Blended Scotch Whisky in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2208 30 71)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (KN 2208 30 71)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch Whisky presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN 2208 30 71.
A single malt whisky produced in Scotland from a malted barley mash by pot still distillation at a single distillery, used as a blending component for Blended Scotch Whisky.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) Scotland origin (ScotlandOriginQuality), (2) production via a blending process (BlendingProcess), and (3) compositional requirement of having both single malt Scotch whisky and single grain Scotch whisky as parts. The container-size split (≤ 2 L for 22083071 vs. > 2 L for 22083079) is captured by the BlendedScotchWhiskySmallContainer subclass using SmallContainer. A coverage gap exists because: (a) the 'other blended whisky' scope note implies exclusion of purely malt-only blends (already covered by BlendedMaltScotchWhisky siblings) and this disjointness is not formally axiomatised here; (b) the minimum ageing and alcoholic strength requirements applicable to Scotch Whisky under EU/UK law are not expressed (the TBox has MinAlcohol40VolQuality but no ageing quality), reducing completeness slightly.
